title: At the Tomb Before Dawn
subtitle: The holy women come with their spices while it is still dark, seeking the Lord in the grave
gospelRef: Matthew 28; Mark 16; Luke 24; John 20 (esp. Matt 28:1; Mark 16:2; Luke 24:1; John 20:1)

stillness: Come now into the quiet of the heart.
Let the spices of prayer be made ready.
Draw near only in peace and stillness of breast.
gospel: In the evening of the sabbath, in the night that follows it,
as the first day of the week began to grow light,
very early in the morning, while it was yet dark,
Mary Magdalene and the other two Marys went,
carrying the spices they had prepared.
And the sun was now rising.
A great stone was rolled against the door of the tomb.
behold: Behold, and attend to each thing as if you were present.
See these holy women, while the darkness still lingers,
rising before the dawn, hastening to the grave.
now, even dead, they render Him the offices of human love.
See in their hands the spices they have prepared,
and in their hearts the whole desire to find their Lord.
Behold the great stone laid against the door —
and know that for every soul some stone stands in the way.
consider: The tomb is the human mind, whatever its state.
The heart of the penitent is called a tomb, by reason of mourning,
for it belongs to the penitent to grieve and to weep over himself.
Consider the stone that blocks the way.
For the penitent, the stone is the proneness to evil;
for those advancing, the difficulty of doing good;
for the contemplative, the heaviness of material things.
Yet this stone, in every state, is most often rolled away
at the mere desire of the devout soul that seeks,
through the grace of the Holy Spirit.
Happy the Mary — that is, the soul —
who comes to the sepulchre of Christ:
to see Him by meditation,
to mourn Him by compassion,
to anoint Him by devotion.
She who first went to sin now first comes to pardon;
she who first took up faithlessness in paradise
now hastens first to take up faith from the sepulchre.
Let us not seek Him in the grave, but in heaven,
where we believe and know that He has ascended.
prayer: Let us therefore imitate these same holy and most devout women,
that as they with their spices sought the Lord
with their whole heart and desire in the sepulchre,
which is the proper place of the dead,
so we too, with our own spices —
may set out with the whole affection of the mind toward God,
and through holy desires seek Him,
not in the grave, but in heaven.

latinJewel.latin: Felix Maria, id est anima, quæ ad sepulchrum Christi venit videre, per meditationem, plangere per compassionem, ac ungere per devotionem!
latinJewel.english: Happy the Mary — that is, the soul — who comes to the tomb of Christ: to see Him through meditation, to mourn Him through compassion, and to anoint Him through devotion!
